description_long: This object is a rectangular, vintage-style radio receiver with a weathered, rusted metal exterior, giving it an aged and industrial appearance. The front face features a large circular speaker area and two prominent round knobs, likely for tuning and volume control. An extendable metal antenna protrudes from the top, angled backward, suggesting functionality for receiving radio signals. The surface is mottled with brown, yellow, and blueish rust patches, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements. The back and sides are plain, with no visible controls or openings, while the bottom appears solid and unadorned. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing durability and function.
description: A rusted, rectangular vintage radio receiver with a large speaker, two knobs, and a metal antenna. Its weathered metal surface shows signs of age and heavy use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the large circular speaker, two round knobs, and the base of the metal antenna.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the plain, rusted metal surface with the antenna visible at the back.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the angled metal antenna and a small round feature on the bottom right, with no visible controls.
description_view_3: The right side view is featureless, showing only the rusted metal surface and the edge of the knobs.
